channel.title: Cross-cultural research on Nils Myszkowski, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in Cross-cultural research on Nils Myszkowski, PhD

item.title: Exploring the Elicitors of Happiness in India
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine exploring the elicitors of happiness in india. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper Go to the journal page for this article here. Download the PDF here.

item.title: A Cross-Cultural Study of Task Specificity in Creativity
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we test the extent to which creativity performance depends on the specific task used, and whether this pattern generalizes across cultures. Abstract The article examines task specificity in creativity by comparing performance patterns across cultural groups. Rather than assuming a single, task-invariant creativity trait, it evaluates how strongly outcomes depend on the task format and demands. Findings support nuanced interpretations of creativity scores and emphasize the need for multi-task assessment designs in cross-cultural work.
